Title: Comprehensive analysis of the BES1 gene family and its expression under abiotic stress and hormone treatment in Populus trichocarpa

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: PtrBES1-7 belongs to group V of the PtrBES1 family; functional characterization in this study is based on transient rather than stable transformation; promoters of all PtrBES1 genes, including PtrBES1-7, contain ABRE and other stress-related cis-elements, supporting their responsiveness to ABA and drought.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. PtrBES1-7 is one of 14 BES1 transcription factors identified in Populus trichocarpa and shows strong induction in leaves and roots by ABA, PEG6000-simulated drought, and NaCl treatments, with the highest expression under drought.
2. Promoter analysis indicates that PtrBES1 family members, including PtrBES1-7, harbor multiple ABA- and stress-responsive cis-elements such as ABRE and MBS, consistent with their stress-inducible expression.
3. Transient overexpression of PtrBES1-7 in P. trichocarpa reduces PEG6000-induced ROS (O_2^- and H_2O_2) accumulation, decreases MDA and EL levels, and increases SOD and POD activities compared with control plants.
4. RNAi silencing of PtrBES1-7 leads to higher ROS, MDA, and EL and lower antioxidant enzyme activities under drought, indicating increased oxidative damage and reduced drought tolerance.
5. Collectively, PtrBES1-7 acts as a positive regulator of drought tolerance in P. trichocarpa by enhancing ROS scavenging and protecting cell membranes from oxidative damage.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: GEO-based tissue expression data from the PopGenIE database were used to generate a PtrBES1 tissue expression heat map indicating stress- and tissue-specific expression (no GEO accession ID specified).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of PtrBES1-7 in Populus trichocarpa enhances tolerance to PEG6000-simulated drought stress by reducing ROS (O_2^- and H_2O_2) accumulation and membrane lipid peroxidation, while RNAi silencing of PtrBES1-7 increases ROS levels, oxidative damage, and drought sensitivity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: PtrBES1-7 positively regulates drought stress tolerance by enhancing antioxidant defense: its overexpression increases SOD and POD activities under drought, leading to reduced accumulation of O_2^- and H_2O_2, lower MDA levels, and decreased EL, thereby limiting membrane lipid peroxidation and preserving cell membrane integrity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling-mediated drought response and ROS/oxidative stress pathway; PtrBES1 family members, including PtrBES1-7, are suggested to function in brassinosteroid and ABA signaling and to act as key transcription factors regulating oxidative stress responses under abiotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BES1_N domain (PF05687) transcription factor of the BES1/BZR1 family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: PtrBES1-7 is strongly induced in both leaves and roots by ABA (200 μM), PEG6000 (7%) drought, and 200 mM NaCl; under PEG6000 drought, PtrBES1-7 shows the highest induction among PtrBES1 genes, with leaf expression increasing to about 10-fold above control at 24 h.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000 (7%) to simulate drought stress; 200 mM NaCl for salt stress; 200 μM ABA for hormone treatment; assays typically performed up to 24–48 h after treatment.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance, especially drought tolerance; hormone (ABA and BR) signaling-related transcriptional regulation; ROS scavenging in Populus trichocarpa.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance; oxidative stress response).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 7% PEG6000 drought for 48 h, MDA content in PtrBES1-7 overexpression leaves is reduced to 0.92 times that of the control, whereas in RNAi lines it increases to 1.15 times the control. After 24 and 48 h of PEG6000 treatment, EL levels in overexpression lines are lower than in controls, with EL at 48 h being 0.65 times the control, while RNAi plants reach 1.26 times the control. At 48 h of PEG6000 treatment, SOD activity in the overexpression line is 1.42 times and POD activity 1.5 times that of the control, whereas RNAi lines show reduced SOD at 24 h and POD reduced to 0.81 times the control at 48 h. Quantitative assays of O_2^- and H_2O_2 show the lowest ROS levels in overexpression lines, intermediate in controls, and highest in RNAi lines.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Populus trichocarpa genotype Nisqually-1 in vitro plantlets; transiently transformed plants carrying 35S:PtrBES1-7 overexpression construct, PtrBES1-7 RNAi construct (pROKII:BES1), or empty pROKII control.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Agrobacterium tumefaciens-mediated transient transformation of P. trichocarpa plantlets; hormone (ABA) and abiotic stress (7% PEG6000, 200 mM NaCl) treatments; RT-qPCR analysis of PtrBES1 gene expression; physiological measurements of MDA and EL; quantification of O_2^- and H_2O_2 contents; DAB and NBT histochemical staining; assays of SOD and POD activities.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression and RNAi knockdown in P. trichocarpa followed by PEG6000-induced drought stress, RT-qPCR verification of transgene expression, physiological assays (MDA, EL, ROS quantification, SOD and POD activities), and histochemical DAB and NBT staining.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Genome-wide HMM search using the BES1_N (PF05687) domain on the Populus trichocarpa genome to identify 14 PtrBES1 genes, followed by cloning of the full-length 996 bp CDS of PtrBES1-7 into pROKII under the CaMV 35S promoter and construction of an inverted-repeat RNAi fragment for functional analysis via transient Agrobacterium-mediated transformation.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: PtrBES1-7 is a drought stress-responsive transcription factor whose overexpression enhances ROS scavenging and drought tolerance in Populus trichocarpa, providing a promising genetic target and theoretical basis for breeding drought-resistant woody plants and improving drought resilience in poplar cultivation.
